title: Lap-Query LambdaMART Blend
summary: Pit calls are locally competitive: on most laps only a small subset of drivers are realistic pit candidates, so learning relative ordering inside each (Year, Race, LapNumber) group can complement global binary AUC models.
rationale: Pit calls are locally competitive: on most laps only a small subset of drivers are realistic pit candidates, so learning relative ordering inside each (Year, Race, LapNumber) group can complement global binary AUC models. Learning-to-rank methods are designed for query-group ordering and are a closer fit to this structure than plain classification alone.
implementation_hint: Train an XGBoost ranker with qid=(Year,Race,LapNumber), using current numerical features plus field-relative features; try `rank:pairwise` and `rank:ndcg`, then blend its OOF scores with the existing binary model via a monotone sigmoid stacker.
expected_effect: Better discrimination among same-lap candidates, especially medium-probability cases where only 1-3 cars are truly in-window.
risk: Many lap groups have zero positives, so grouping and weighting need care; standalone ranker may underperform if blended improperly.
